items.title: Report: Penix, Njoku among targets used in fraud
items.body_text: A former Alabama defensive end is accused of using wigs and fake IDs to impersonate NFL players to secure nearly $20 million in fraudulent loans, with a report identifying Michael Penix Jr., David Njoku and Xavier McKinney as the targeted players.
items.author: Michael Rothstein

items.title: Chargers GM shuts down Johnston trade rumors
items.body_text: Chargers general manager Joe Hortiz shut down trade rumors surrounding wide receiver Quentin Johnston on Thursday.
items.author: Kris Rhim

items.title: Chiefs GM expecting a lot of trades in first round
items.body_text: Chiefs general manager Brett Veach said Thursday that there will "probably be a lot of trades" in the first round of this year's NFL draft.
items.author: Nate Taylor

items.title: Titans QB Ward on track to throw at minicamp
items.body_text: Quarterback Cam Ward, who injured his throwing shoulder in the Titans' season finale, is trending toward throwing at minicamp in June, general manager Mike Borgonzi says.
items.author: Turron Davenport

items.title: Memo: NFL onboarding replacement officials
items.body_text: The NFL has started onboarding potential replacement officials as the expiration of its CBA with the NFL Referees Association approaches.
items.author: Kevin Seifert

items.title: Dolphins GM: RB Achane 'not available' for trade
items.body_text: Dolphins running back De'Von Achane is "not available" for a trade, general manager Jon-Eric Sullivan said Wednesday, as the team looks to extend its homegrown star.
items.author: Marcel Louis-Jacques

items.title: WR Knox found negligent in crash, to pay $2.8M
items.body_text: A Texas court on Wednesday issued a default judgment of more than $2.8 million against Theodore Knox, the co-defendant in one of the civil lawsuits against Chiefs receiver Rashee Rice stemming from a 2024 street racing crash in Dallas.
items.author: Michael Rothstein and Nate Taylor

items.title: Russini resigns from Athletic following Vrabel pics
items.body_text: NFL reporter Dianna Russini has resigned from The Athletic less than a week after published photos of her and Patriots coach Mike Vrabel at an Arizona resort prompted an internal investigation at the New York Times-owned sports outlet.
